title: General form
text: If "F varies jointly with the square of v and with A" then the general form of our relationship will be $$F={kv}^2 A$$.

title: Finding k
text: The constant k can be found by dividing F by $$v^2 A$$.

title: Evaluating k
text: $$k=\frac{F}{v^2 A}$$. If $$F=15$$ when $$A=40$$ and $$v=20$$, what is k?

title: Finding new force
text: Now we know our relationship is $$F=\frac{3}{3200} v^2 A$$. To find the new force on a surface with an area of $$65$$ sq. ft. and velocity $$30$$, just plug in $$A=65$$ and $$v=30!$$ Round to two decimal places.
